mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that raises discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate threshold, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment (UNRATE) and gains in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signal a tightening labor market, driving positive S&P 500 returns via corporate earnings optimism over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or the directional relationship flips in 40%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, boosting corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO shows no correlation with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or the relationship reverses in 50%+ of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 2,100 thousand units signals stronger consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of cases where HOUST exceeds 2100, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2% erodes real returns, prompting central bank intervention that destabilizes equity markets and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL shows no predictive power for SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or the directional relationship flips in 40%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicates aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, creating headwinds for equities and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or the combined signal underperforms standalone proxies.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 4.5% signals labor market strength, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of cases where UNRATE falls below 4.5%, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust economic demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through sustained corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and HOUST fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or the combined signal underperforms standalone proxies.
